remarks.name: Tigera Blog (Calico / AI Security)
remarks.role: AI agent governance, security, accountability frameworks, enterprise AI deployment policy.
remarks.text: NVIDIA OpenShell Secures the Agent. Who Governs the Fleet?
remarks.content: In short: At GTC 2026, NVIDIA released OpenShell, an open source runtime that sandboxes autonomous AI agents with kernel-level policy: what files they can touch, what processes they can spawn, where their traffic can go.... The post NVIDIA OpenShell Secures the Agent. Who Governs the Fleet? appeared
remarks.contentTranslation: 简而言之：在GTC 2026上，NVIDIA发布了OpenShell，这是一个开源运行时，通过内核级策略对自主AI代理进行沙盒化隔离：它们可以访问哪些文件、可以生成哪些进程、流量可以流向何处……《NVIDIA OpenShell 守护代理安全，谁来管理整个集群？》一文已发布。
remarks.translation: NVIDIA OpenShell 守护智能体安全。谁来管理整个智能体集群？
remarks.observation: 这条言论可作为 Agent 产品形态、协作方式或能力边界的观察入口；只代表公开观点，不作为公司事实主证据。

remarks.name: Simon Willison's Blog
remarks.role: AI coding tools, LLM release notes, prompt engineering, datasette ecosystem, developer workflow observations.
remarks.text: Reverse-engineering is cheap now
remarks.content: I keep hearing anecdotes from people who used coding agents to reverse-engineer and automate devices in their homes. I think this is an interesting illustration of the impact of the reduced cost of writing code. Prior to agents, it was entirely possible to reverse-engineer home devi
remarks.contentTranslation: 我不断听到有人利用编程代理逆向工程并自动化家中设备的故事。我认为这生动地说明了编写代码成本降低所带来的影响。在代理出现之前，对家用设备进行逆向工程是完全可行的，
remarks.translation: 逆向工程现在成本很低。

remarks.name: Aaron Levie
remarks.role: ceo box - your business lives in content. unleash it with AI
remarks.text:
In a world where there are strong open source alternatives that are only just behind the frontier models, you make yourself less secure and competitive by gatekeeping access to frontier model capabilities.

If you play this out, even if America could fully ban access to open source models, other ecosystems wouldn't have that ban and they'd have a leg up on access to those models. They could use them against us -or just use them to better defend themselves- and our own companies wouldn't be able to keep up.

The calculus of what you do about AI regulation simply must change to account for the fact that open weights models are far stronger than many expected.
remarks.translation: 在一个拥有强大开源替代方案、且这些方案仅略逊于前沿模型的世界里，通过限制前沿模型能力的获取，你只会让自己变得更不安全、更缺乏竞争力。推演下去，即便美国能完全禁止开源模型的获取，其他生态系统却不会有这样的禁令，它们在获取这些模型方面将占据优势。它们可能利用这些模型来对付我们——或者仅仅用来更好地自我防御——而我们自己的企业却无法跟上步伐。关于人工智能监管的考量，必须因应一个事实而改变：开放权重模型的强大程度远超许多人的预期。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察模型运行、算力、存储、sandbox 或开发平台基础设施的变化；后续事实判断需回到原始公告或技术文档。

remarks.name: Guillermo Rauch
remarks.role: vercel CEO
remarks.text: Thankful to 🇦🇷 Argentina for once again giving it all. Grit, passion, talent, faith. To Messi for proving he’s the best ever. And to America 🇺🇸 for hosting such a wonderful event. Enhorabuena tíos 🇪🇸!
remarks.translation: 感谢🇦🇷阿根廷再次全力以赴。坚韧、激情、天赋、信念。感谢梅西证明了他是有史以来最伟大的球员。也感谢美国🇺🇸主办了如此精彩的活动。恭喜了兄弟们🇪🇸！
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

remarks.name: Aaron Levie
remarks.role: ceo box - your business lives in content. unleash it with AI
remarks.text:
A lot of people make the mistake of thinking that when AI costs drop, that spend on AI drops with it. Usually the opposite happens. 

When you make AI cheaper, it will get consumed more. Because now you can afford to use for a wider of tasks than you did before. You start to write more code. You review more code for bugs and security. You run agents on large data sets you couldn’t process before. And so on.

Thus, for the foreseeable future, anything that lowers the cost of tokens will drive up inference demand.

This also gives you some insight also into why even open source business models work in AI. No one is running these models on their devices; they’re running them in infra. Great time to be one of those providers.
remarks.translation: 很多人误以为，当AI成本下降时，AI支出也会随之减少。但通常情况恰恰相反。当AI变得更便宜时，它的使用量反而会增加。因为现在你有能力将它用于比以往更广泛的任务。你会开始编写更多代码，审查更多代码中的漏洞和安全问题，在以前无法处理的大型数据集上运行智能代理，等等。因此，在可预见的未来，任何降低token成本的因素都会推高推理需求。这也让你多少能理解，为什么即使是开源商业模式在AI领域也能行得通。没有人是在自己的设备上运行这些模型，他们都是在基础设施上运行的。现在正是成为这类服务提供商的绝佳时机。
remarks.observation: 这条言论可作为 Agent 产品形态、协作方式或能力边界的观察入口；只代表公开观点，不作为公司事实主证据。

remarks.name: Guillermo Rauch
remarks.role: vercel CEO
remarks.text:
Incidentally, I’m very bullish on cybersecurity as one of the best benchmarks for superintelligence. The “IQ test” of software engineering.

The best engineers I’ve worked with in my career have usually had a deep background or interest in security.

It’s actually easy for a model to “one-shot an XYZ clone” and impress people on X. But that’s not a good test.

Finding, patching, reversing, and exploiting require a cognitive skill that transcends programming languages, runtimes, frameworks… It demands true reasoning power from the model and “corner thinking”. Very, very few humans excel at this, let alone in ordinary day-to-day software writing.

Seeing Kimi K3 do so well here bodes well for open models.
remarks.translation: 顺便说一句，我非常看好网络安全，认为它是衡量超级智能的最佳基准之一，堪称软件工程领域的“智商测试”。我职业生涯中合作过的最优秀的工程师，通常都对安全领域有深厚的背景或浓厚的兴趣。一个模型确实很容易“一键生成某个应用的克隆版”，在X平台上让人眼前一亮，但这并不是一个好的测试标准。漏洞的发现、修补、逆向分析和利用，需要一种超越编程语言、运行时环境和框架的认知能力……它要求模型具备真正的推理能力和“边缘思维”。在这方面，极少数人类能够出类拔萃，更不用说在日常普通的软件编写中了。看到Kimi K3在此表现如此出色，对开放模型来说是个好兆头。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 AI 编程工具、设计到代码流程和软件生产方式的变化；需要结合产品发布或文档再做事实判断。

remarks.role: Claude Code anthropicai.   prev YC W20, southpkcommons, medialab

remarks.translation: 正在进一步撰写一篇关于我们在此过程中所学到的经验，以及如何将这些经验应用到你的技能和系统提示中的文章。https://t.co/ChfVbKEGic
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

remarks.name: Aaron Levie
remarks.role: ceo box - your business lives in content. unleash it with AI
remarks.text:
Good post if you’re trying to understand AI diffusion. Progress driven by AI will ultimately be rate limited by its interaction with the real world. 

The reason coding, for instance, has been adopted so quickly is you can write a near infinite amount of code, test it, and run it -and it can add value- without anyone in the outside world ever having to do anything differently. A single person, from a computer, can just make something work end-to-end differently.

This is not true for life sciences, where new drug development eventually needs to be tested for years. Doing a sale, which requires going back and forth with a prospect. Or even a contract, which has to be negotiated on the other side by your counterparty. 

“An AI will hand you a genuinely clever design for a jet turbine blade. It might be far more likely to work than anything your engineers came up with. It'll still probably fail, because that's the base rate at the edge of what anyone knows. The only way to find out is to build the blade and try to break it.

That's the real limit on learning, and it doesn't care how smart you are. Coming up with ideas was never the hard part. The hard part is how fast reality answers them.”

Incidentally, this is why you need an applied AI that actually takes intelligence and makes it useful within the workflows of existing industries. Model outputs -alone- are not enough in most cases. You need to actually change the underlying workflows, and you need systems to deal with the realities of the real world feedback loops that are in these industries.

This is why there’s so much opportunity in the applied AI layer.
remarks.translation: 如果你正试图理解人工智能的扩散，这是一篇不错的帖子。人工智能推动的进步最终将受限于它与现实世界的互动速度。以编程为例，它之所以能迅速被采纳，是因为你可以编写近乎无限的代码，进行测试和运行——并且它能创造价值——而无需外界任何人做出任何改变。一个人，只需一台电脑，就能从头到尾让某件事以不同的方式运作。但这在生命科学领域并不成立，新药开发最终需要经过多年的测试。做一笔销售，需要与潜在客户反复沟通。甚至一份合同，也得由你的交易对手方进行谈判。“人工智能可能会给你一个真正巧妙的喷气涡轮叶片设计。它可能比你的工程师想出的任何方案都更有可能成功。但它仍然很可能会失败，因为这是任何人所知边缘的基本概率。唯一能验证的方法就是制造出叶片并尝试破坏它。这才是学习的真正限制，而这与你的聪明程度无关。提出想法从来都不是最困难的部分。最困难的部分是现实能多快给出答案。”顺便说一句，这就是为什么你需要一种应用型人工智能，它能真正利用智能，并在现有行业的工作流程中使其发挥作用。在大多数情况下，仅靠模型输出是不够的。你需要真正改变底层工作流程，并且需要系统来应对这些行业中现实世界反馈循环的实际情况。这就是应用型人工智能层存在如此多机遇的原因。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 AI 编程工具、设计到代码流程和软件生产方式的变化；需要结合产品发布或文档再做事实判断。

remarks.name: Zara Zhang
remarks.role: Builder. Dangerously skips permissions. Harvard’17. GitHub: https://t.co/KCuEajezlL YouTube: https://t.co/8xzbGWtf6w
remarks.text:
We need to get used to the idea that code/software can now be disposable

Some examples (all are discarded after usage):
1. Creating design playgrounds/modals for yourself to fine-tune the look and feel of a design
2. Creating HTML pages to help you understand the code
3. Spinning up a throwaway dashboard to inspect something once
remarks.translation:
我们需要接受一个观念：代码/软件现在可以是“一次性”的。以下是一些例子（使用后即丢弃）：  
1. 为自己创建设计沙盒/模态框，以微调设计的视觉与交互感受  
2. 创建HTML页面来帮助理解代码逻辑  
3. 临时搭建一个用完即弃的仪表盘，仅用于一次性检查某些内容
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 AI 编程工具、设计到代码流程和软件生产方式的变化；需要结合产品发布或文档再做事实判断。

remarks.name: Zara Zhang
remarks.role: Builder. Dangerously skips permissions. Harvard’17. GitHub: https://t.co/KCuEajezlL YouTube: https://t.co/8xzbGWtf6w
remarks.text:
Whenever someone asks me "I'm just getting started with posting content on social media; what should I talk about?"

My answer is: What are the top 3 questions that you get asked most frequently by friends/acquaintances/colleagues? Turn those answers into videos/posts. Just say it like how you said it to them in real life. That is your positioning.

If you have repeatedly said something for >3 times, it's worth turning into a video

One of my initial motivations for posting on social media was because I was tired of repeating myself in real life
remarks.translation: 每当有人问我“我刚准备开始在社交媒体上发内容，该聊些什么呢？”我的回答是：朋友、熟人、同事最常问你的三个问题是什么？把那些回答变成视频或帖子。就像你在现实生活中对他们说的那样去表达。这就是你的定位。如果你重复说过某件事超过3次，那就值得把它做成视频。我最初在社交媒体上发帖的动机之一，就是因为厌倦了在现实生活中一遍遍重复自己说过的话。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

remarks.text: Claude Code uses Bun written in Rust now
remarks.content: In Rewriting Bun in Rust Jarred Sumner made the following claim: Claude Code v2.1.181 (released June 17th) and later use the Rust port of Bun. Startup got 10% faster on Linux but otherwise, barely anyone noticed. Boring is good.</
remarks.contentTranslation: 在《用 Rust 重写 Bun》一文中，Jarred Sumner 提出了以下观点：Claude Code v2.1.181（于6月17日发布）及后续版本使用了 Bun 的 Rust 移植版。启动速度在 Linux 上提升了10%，但除此之外几乎无人察觉。平淡无奇，反而是好事。
remarks.translation: Claude Code 现在使用基于 Rust 编写的 Bun。

remarks.name: Simon Willison's Blog
remarks.role: AI coding tools, LLM release notes, prompt engineering, datasette ecosystem, developer workflow observations.
remarks.text: Quoting Armin Ronacher
remarks.content: The shared language of a software project is not English or Python but it is the common understanding of what its concepts mean, where the boundaries are, which invariants matter, who owns what, and why the system has t
remarks.contentTranslation: 软件项目的共同语言并非英语或Python，而是对其概念含义、边界划分、关键不变性、所有权归属以及系统如此设计的原因所形成的共识。
remarks.translation: 引用 Armin Ronacher 的观点

podcasts.name: AI & I by Every
podcasts.title: 打造一所让AI模型学习人性的学校
podcasts.originalTitle: Building a School Where AI Models Learn About Humanity
podcasts.excerpt: 如果规模定律成立——Surge AI首席执行官Edwin Chen相信它们确实成立——我们正飞速冲向一个未来，在那里，人类能做的任何事，AI都能做得更好。当OpenAI的模型运用新颖的代数几何技术，推翻了数学家Paul Erdős提出的一个开放猜想时，菲尔兹奖得主Timothy Gowers敏锐地感受到了这种转变。他起初以为模型证明了一个上界，并做好了心理准备：那将意味着“数学家很快就要完蛋了”。当他意识到模型只是找到了一个反例时，他松了口气——这为他争取了一两年时间，让他毕生投入的事业不至于那么快就被AI做得更好。作为为各大模型公司提供训练模型所用的数据环境和评估系统的公司创始人和CEO，Chen对AI模型吸收那些我们曾视为人类专属任务的速度，有着独特的视角……
podcasts.originalExcerpt: If scaling laws hold—and Surge AI CEO Edwin Chen believes they do—we’re hurtling toward a future where there’s nothing humans can do that AI can’t do better. When OpenAI’s models disproved an open conjecture posed by mathematician Paul Erdős using novel algebraic geometry techniques, Fields medalist Timothy Gowers felt the shift acutely. He initially thought the model had proved an upper bound, and braced himself: that would mean it was “all over for mathematicians very soon.” When he realized it had only found a counterexample, he was relieved—it bought him another year or two before the thing he’s devoted his life to becomes something AI does better.As founder and CEO of the company behind the data environments and evals the major model companies use to train their models, Chen has a unique perspective on how quickly AI models are absorbing tasks we used to think of as uniqu…
